skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Perform code reviews following Sentry engineering practices. Use when reviewing pull requests, examining code changes, or providing feedback on code quality. Covers security, performance, testing, and design review.
skill.summary: Perform code reviews following Sentry engineering practices. Use when reviewing pull requests, exami...
skill.author: getsentry

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown review guidance, illustrative code examples, and a documentation link, with no executable skill logic or prompt injection attempt found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Illustrative Code Blocks
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged fenced Markdown examples as shell or command execution. Lines 67-98 contain Python and TypeScript examples that teach code review patterns and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside Markdown code fences under common patterns to flag. The skill contains no script file, command runner, or instruction to execute these examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Link
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a hardcoded URL. The URL is a reference link to Sentry code review guidelines and does not perform a network request or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in the References section as a Markdown link. No evidence found of code that fetches the URL or sends repository data to it.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Review Vocabulary
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance terms in prose. The referenced lines describe review topics such as security vulnerabilities, design assessment, and SQL injection examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is ordinary code review guidance and a negative SQL injection example. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, host scanning, or network reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss security, performance, and design risks when feedback lacks structure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Sentry-style review checks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews pull requests for runtime errors, performance risks, and side effects.
Checks Django ORM changes for query and N+1 performance issues.
Reviews React useEffect usage for missing dependencies and stale data risks.
Flags security concerns such as injection, XSS, access control gaps, and exposed secrets.
Assesses whether tests cover requirements, edge cases, and critical user paths.
Prompts senior review for schema, API, framework, and security-sensitive changes.
limitations: It provides review guidance and does not run tests or static analysis by itself.
It is tailored to Sentry engineering practices and may need adaptation for other teams.
It depends on the model having access to the relevant diff, context, and project conventions.
It cannot verify runtime behavior without project execution results or logs.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Risk Review
use_cases.description: Review a pull request for correctness, side effects, security concerns, and missing tests before merge.

use_cases.title: Django Performance Review
use_cases.description: Check backend changes for ORM query issues, N+1 patterns, and changes that need broader design review.

use_cases.title: Review Quality Coaching
use_cases.description: Help reviewers write polite, actionable comments that separate blocking risks from style preferences.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small diff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request using the Sentry code-review skill. Focus on correctness, tests, security, performance, and clear actionable feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Django Query Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Backend change that touches models, queries, or serializers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Django change for ORM performance, N+1 queries, migration risk, backwards compatibility, and missing test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: React Behavior Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Frontend change involving hooks, state, or data fetching.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React change for missing dependencies, stale state, side effects, user-visible regressions, and test gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Senior Risk Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for broad architectural or security-sensitive changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this change for long-term design impact, API contract risk, security-sensitive behavior, performance-critical paths, and issues that need senior engineer review.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds a database lookup inside a loop over users.
output: Finding: The loop may create an N+1 query pattern.
Impact: This can slow down pages as the number of users grows.
Suggestion: Prefetch the related profile data and add a regression test for query count.

output_examples.input: A React component fetches data in an effect but omits a changing user identifier from dependencies.
output: Finding: The effect may use stale input when the selected user changes.
Impact: The component can show outdated data.
Suggestion: Include the user identifier in the dependency list and test user switching behavior.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes an API response field without a migration plan.
output: Finding: The API contract change may break existing clients.
Impact: Consumers can fail after deployment.
Suggestion: Add a compatibility path, document the migration, and request senior review.

best_practices: Provide the full diff and relevant project context before requesting review.
Ask for findings ordered by severity with clear evidence and suggested fixes.
Share test results or known constraints so the review can separate risk from preference.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for running tests, linters, or security scanners.
Do not ask for style-only feedback when the review goal is risk reduction.
Do not provide isolated snippets when behavior depends on surrounding code.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews code changes for correctness, security, performance, testing, design fit, and long-term maintenance risks.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill and does not run commands, tests, or network requests.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it only for Sentry projects?
faq.answer: It is based on Sentry engineering practices, but many review checks apply to similar software projects.

faq.question: Can it find every security issue?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure review, but dedicated security testing and human review remain important.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the diff, related files, test results, expected behavior, and any project-specific constraints.
